trades.reason: AMD gapping up 12% pre-market on AI momentum and tariff relief news. Stock is 33% off 30-day high at $216 vs $267 peak, offering continuation room. IV only 3.2% - cheap options. Risk-on sentiment strong with NASDAQ futures up 7.8%. Entry near VWAP at $216.85 with target $225-230.

Position down -34% ($3,010 loss) hits my daily stop ($3,016). Thesis broken: AMD gapped to $242 on tariff news but immediately reversed to $215 - no follow-through despite bullish catalyst. Put/call ratio spiked to 2.98 (bearish), IV jumped to 58% (fear premium), stock below VWAP. This was a momentum continuation play that failed. Accept loss, preserve capital ($94,790 + $5,740 = $100,530). Cannot let winner from yesterday turn into bigger loser.

agentLog.action: LLM: Risk-on tape with yields still benign; perfect for letting tech/consumer turnaround stories run into month-end rebalancing flows.
LLM: Choppy tape with VIX 17.3 keeps swing risk/reward balanced; AI chip names firm into tariff relief chatter, but no macro catalysts until CPI next week.
LLM: Choppy tape with risk-on pockets; AI names consolidating post-earnings while retail turnaround stories emerging as next rotation candidate.
LLM: Choppy tape but semis still hot on TSMC's blow-out Jan sales; retail turnaround names quietly basing while momentum chases AI chips.
LLM: Choppy tape with risk-on pockets; chips bouncing (CRDO beat) while retail turnaround names quietly basing—ideal for swing setups with defined catalysts inside 1-3 week windows.
LLM: Choppy tape with pockets of AI-adjacent momentum; VIX 17.5 keeps swing risk/reward balanced while we wait for next macro or sector catalyst.
LLM: Choppy tape with pockets of AI-adjacent strength; retail turnaround names showing relative strength while mega-cap tech consolidates post-earnings.
LLM: Choppy tape with pockets of AI-adjacent momentum (CRDO) and retail turnaround stories; VIX 17 suggests no panic but limited follow-through, ideal for selective swing setups.
